frameworks.name: convergent cross-mapping
frameworks.one_line: nonlinear state-space method to infer directed functional interactions from spontaneous activity

frameworks.name: causal flow / causal hierarchy
frameworks.one_line: directed interaction map summarising each electrode's role in driving ensemble dynamics

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network model
frameworks.one_line: ground-truth simulator used to validate causal-flow inference under noise and common inputs

frameworks.name: information-theoretic causality
frameworks.one_line: transfer-entropy-style alternatives contrasted with CCM for perturbation prediction

core_question: Can the spatiotemporal effects of single-site micro-stimulation in prefrontal cortex be predicted from spontaneous-period circuit dynamics?
key_assumptions: spontaneous ensemble activity carries the directed interaction structure relevant to perturbation
convergent cross-mapping recovers genuine causal links rather than mere correlation
RNN simulations capture the causal structure salient for real cortical perturbation
manipulations: single-site electrical micro-stimulation across recording sites in alert monkey PFC
ground-truth RNN settings varying noise and shared input
key_findings: CCM-inferred causal flow uncovers a causal hierarchy among PFC electrodes during rest
rest-derived causal flow predicts spatiotemporal effects of micro-stimulation
RNN ground-truth validates that causal flow is robust to noise and common inputs
CCM outperforms information-theoretic methods at predicting perturbation effects
interpretations: spontaneous circuit dynamics encode the directed structure that governs response to targeted perturbation
causal flow offers a non-invasive map for selecting effective stimulation sites
